title: EoE Video and Theatrical Difference
wikitext:
[]: EoE Video and Theatrical Difference             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 17, 2005 7:04 am Here's an interesting tidbit from MDWig's site. Turns out there was a video version of EoE released in Japan with an interesting difference. The Video Version of Episode 26': Title screen: "Neon Genesis Evangelion" In the kitchen scene Asuka's "Iya" (No) is written on the screen, we don't hear it. Eye catch: "Neon Genesis Evangelion One More Final: I need you." The caption at the conclusion of the episode says simply "End" Credits for just episode 26' are shown at the end to a string version of "Jesus bleibet meine Freude" The Theatrical Version of Episode 26': Title screen: "The End of Evangelion" In the kitchen scene Asuka's "Iya" (liar) is said and not written on the screen. Eye catch: "The End of Evangelion One More Final: I need you." The caption at the conclusion of the episode says "End of the movie" There are no ending credits, just a black screen.

[]: Re: EoE Video and Theatrical Difference             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 17, 2005 9:10 am Down the path of the Dark Side, this overanalysis is. I dunno if you noticed, but those aren't the only two times Anno has used telop in NGE. You forgot Lilith's "Okaeri nasai", for one thing. And you finally got around to seeing EoTV, so I needn't go any further. ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Does anyone have a digital copy of the scene with the caption instead of the spoken "No"?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 17, 2005 9:27 am I just wondered why the director would choose captions in the video release for this paticularly important line. It might say something about the meaning he'd like to ascribe to the line, or give insight into other areas where captions are used. This doesn't seem more overanalysing than looking into production shots or the script?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 17, 2005 10:45 am I think it's better spoken aloud. Having it as a subtitle, while counter-conventional, doesn't really add much. By being plain text, we don't really get a sense in how that line is delivered. Shinji is so enraged by this line, and not being able to hear how it is delivered would certainly hinder the whole movie, as that is one of the pivotal moments in the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jun 17, 2005 11:45 am well... if you know that the only difference is going to be the "no" in script across the front of the screen, can you then draw conclusions on your own? and i agree with EM's view that the method with which the "no" is conveyed is also important to the scene (with disgust? with anger? with sadness?), though i have the dub, so i dont know what it really sounds like

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 17, 2005 12:02 pm Yes, I suppose a bold faced caption "Iya", while perhaps more striking, could never really convey what the voice actors tones bring across. So why was it put in the video release?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jun 17, 2005 12:15 pm i, for one, have no idea at all about the reasons for this difference. was the video release at a different time than the dvd release? perhaps it was a simple matter of anno deciding to change it afterwards...

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Fri Jun 17, 2005 6:23 pm Perhaps it was intended to more clearly establish rejection as a theme, and the same could be said for the other instances in which text appears as well. I guess they decided something about it wasn't working, and changed it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 17, 2005 6:33 pm Perhaps it was simply Anno experimenting and using the video release purchasers as his unwitting guniea pigs. How did Asuka's line play in the theatres? Resounding in full bass on all speakers?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jun 17, 2005 11:12 pm wait... why would he try that out on video-buyers? it doesnt make much sense...though it does provide a base with which he can test and ask questions, it still does not really account for the discrepancy anyone know what his mental state was at the time?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Jun 18, 2005 2:28 am Likely the spoken word was removed in the video version as a gesture of making a theatrical viewing distinct from it, thereby superemphasizing the importance of the word. The word is so pivotal and harsh, one cannot even hear it after the film has left theaters. So much for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Sat Jun 18, 2005 3:17 am In my opinion, it does not change the meaning of the word or what it meant to the story. Emphasis is like a girl's skirt. It can be long or short.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Jun 18, 2005 5:08 am Compare: Shinji (weakly - getting to his feet): Help me... Somebody... Somebody help me... Help me... Help me... Me... Help ME!! (throws the table aside) Don't leave me alone! Don't abandon me! Don't kill me! (picks up a chair and throws it at Asuka's feet) Asuka: (Screen text: "No" ) (Shinji strangles Asuka - Komm Susser Tod (Come Sweet Death) begins to play) and... Shinji: It'd be better if I wasn't here either. So I should just die, too. Rei: Then, why are you here? Â (Shinji as a child, crying with a large travel bag next to him) Shinji: Is it okay for me to be here? (Screen text: "Silence" - Fades out) Shinji (screaming): NOOOOOOOOOOO!! I think in the Video version, Anno was trying to emphasize a thematic connection between these two instances: Asuka's cold rejection of Shinji's plea for help, i.e. for her to "save" him*, and the indifference of the universe to his fundamental question "Is it okay for me to be here?". Shinji's rage, in the one case, and despair, in the other, manifest as an overwelming flood of "destrudo", and the real world result is "everybody just dies" *(#25) Misato: That's the result you wanted. Shinji: I wanted? Rei: Yes. Destruction, the world where nobody was saved. Shinji: NO. It's that nobody saved me . Ritsuko: Nobody can save you. This is another case of something added to the Genesis version being removed for the Renewal version (the prime example being the background images in the Requarium in #23')

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Sat Jun 18, 2005 5:34 am Shin-seiki wrote: This is another case of something added to the Genesis version being removed for the Renewal version (the prime example being the background images in the Requarium in #23')

[]: Re: EoE Video and Theatrical Difference             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 9:05 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Does anyone have a digital copy of the scene with the caption instead of the spoken "No"?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Jun 23, 2005 11:25 am ^^ That post so totally rules... Thanx!

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Thu Jun 23, 2005 12:05 pm Awesome! It really does take a away from the scene when it's just captioned.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 12:16 pm I live to serve.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 23, 2005 12:39 pm Thanks for the link SP! Ugh! This wasn't what I thought at all. I thought it was a fullscreen white on black caption, as with the later caption. It isn't. It's effectively a sub placed at the top left hand corner of the screen, and flashes for the briefest of moments. I'm glad this didn't make it into the DVD release. The scene really is better without it. I give thanks to the legions of jananese fans whose hard earned yen was sacraficed so that we may have a better DVD release.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jun 23, 2005 2:54 pm And here's a screencap in case anyone didn't want to dick about with my terrible bandwidth.
